name: Danville Redevelopment And Housing Authority
description_short: Public housing authority providing affordable housing and rental assistance to low-income residents across Danville and surrounding Virginia counties.
description_long:
The Danville Redevelopment and Housing Authority (DRHA) is a government agency established to serve low-income households in Danville, Virginia, and surrounding areas. As a public housing authority, DRHA operates under HUD oversight and focuses on expanding affordable housing access to vulnerable populations. The organization has been managing public housing developments and rental assistance programs for residents who qualify based on income requirements.

DRHA operates two primary programs: direct public housing through 563 units across 7 multi-family developments, and the Housing Choice Voucher program (Section 8), administering approximately 1,600 vouchers in Danville, Martinsville, and Pittsylvania and Henry Counties. Beyond housing placement, DRHA provides resident services including GED classes, book club programming, and domestic violence support sessions. The organization employs approximately 41 staff members and serves roughly 1,600 residents in public housing with an additional 1,600 voucher holders in the tri-county area.

DRHA distinguishes itself through comprehensive resident support services that go beyond basic housing provision. The authority maintains transparent governance with public board meetings and publishes detailed planning documents including Annual Plans, Capital Fund 5-Year Plans, and administrative policies. Their community programming demonstrates a commitment to resident stability and self-sufficiency through educational and social support services.

As a government agency, DRHA is accountable to federal HUD standards and local oversight, ensuring compliance with fair housing laws and transparent operations. However, as a public authority, eligibility requirements and unit availability may be limited, and the organization cannot provide direct financial counseling or credit repair services. Prospective residents should verify current waitlist status and income qualification thresholds.
pros: Operates 563 public housing units across 7 developments serving approximately 1,600 residents
Administers 1,600 Housing Choice Vouchers across multi-county region (Danville, Martinsville, Pittsylvania, Henry Counties)
Offers free resident services including GED classes and educational programming
Provides domestic violence support sessions and Book Buds community programs
Publishes transparent planning documents (Annual Plans, Capital Fund 5-Year Plans, ACOP, Admin Plans)
Government accountability through HUD oversight and public board meetings
Serves approximately 3,200 households combining public housing and voucher programs
cons: Public housing likely has extended waitlists with limited unit availability
Strict income eligibility requirements may exclude moderate-income households
Cannot provide credit counseling, credit repair, or financial literacy services
Geographic service area limited to Danville and surrounding Virginia counties
As a housing-only provider, cannot assist with debt, loans, or credit issues
best_for: Low-income households seeking affordable rental housing in Danville, Virginia area
Families and individuals needing housing stability combined with community services
Residents interested in GED programs, adult education, or domestic violence support
Renters eligible for Housing Choice Vouchers in Martinsville or Pittsylvania counties
diagnosis: DRHA is essential for low-income households seeking affordable housing in the Danville, Virginia area and surrounding tri-county region. The critical caveat is that DRHA only provides housing solutions—they cannot assist with credit repair, debt relief, financial counseling, or loan products. Applicants must meet strict income eligibility and navigate public housing waitlists.
services: Housing Choice Voucher (Section 8) rental assistance administration
Annual planning and capital improvement planning
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
